pathway_applied: Pathway 1: Persistent symptoms after conservative management

decomposed_criteria.criterion_text: Low back pain or radicular symptoms persist for at least 6 weeks
Symptoms have not meaningfully improved with conservative treatment
Imaging results are expected to affect management
Neurologic deficit or red flag symptoms documented

extracted_facts.fact_text: Lower back pain with left leg radiation for 9 weeks
extracted_facts.source_quote: lower back pain radiating to the left leg for 9 weeks

extracted_facts.fact_text: Completed 6 weeks of physical therapy and NSAIDs
extracted_facts.source_quote: Completed 6 weeks of physical therapy and NSAIDs

extracted_facts.fact_text: Decreased left ankle reflex and positive straight-leg raise
extracted_facts.source_quote: decreased left ankle reflex and positive straight-leg raise

extracted_facts.fact_text: MRI may guide epidural steroid injection planning
extracted_facts.source_quote: considering epidural steroid injection depending on MRI findings

criteria_evaluation.explanation: Documented symptom duration exceeds the 6-week threshold.
Conservative treatment trial is explicitly documented.
Note states minimal improvement after conservative care.

criteria_evaluation.patient_evidence: Considering epidural injection based on MRI
criteria_evaluation.explanation: Provider links MRI findings to injection planning.

criteria_evaluation.patient_evidence: Decreased ankle reflex, positive straight-leg raise
criteria_evaluation.explanation: Objective neurologic findings are documented on exam.

rationale: Pathway 1 is satisfied: symptoms exceed 6 weeks, conservative therapy was attempted without meaningful improvement, neurologic findings are documented, and imaging is linked to injection planning.
policy_citations: Pathway 1: Persistent symptoms after conservative management
